mnemonics: Linked List Lasso: Imagine lassoing nodes together, each node pointing to the next, to remember that self-referential structures contain pointers to their own type.
keyPoints: A self-referential structure contains one or more pointers to a structure of the same type.
They are the fundamental building blocks for dynamic data structures like linked lists, trees, and graphs.
Memory for these structures is typically allocated dynamically at runtime using functions like malloc().
commonMistakes: Forgetting to set the next/pointer member to NULL for the last node, causing dangling pointers.
Incorrectly managing memory, leading to leaks (not freeing allocated nodes) or accessing freed memory.
